steps.title: Identify the boundary line
steps.content: Use the answer choices as shortcuts. The boundary line in the graph crosses the $y$-axis at $6$ and slopes downward steeply, so the only possible boundary is $y=-4x+6$. That leaves those choices.

steps.title: Determine the inequality direction
steps.content: The shaded region is above the boundary line. A point above the line, such as $(0,8)$, satisfies $8\gt -4(0)+6$. Therefore the inequality uses $\gt $, not $\lt$.

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A) $y\lt -4x+6$: correct line but the shading is above, not below.
  • B) $y\lt 6x-4$: slope $6$ and y-intercept $-4$ do not match the boundary.
  • C) $y\gt -4x+6$: correct line and correct direction (above).
  • D) $y\gt 6x-4$: wrong slope and wrong y-intercept.
That confirms C is correct.
